title: Control of 3' splice site selection by the yeast splicing factor Fyv6
author: Lipinski, Karli
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: R code for calculation of FAnS values from S. cerevisiae RNA-seq data and subsequent data visualization. Inputs are featureCounts jcounts outputs for each sample (text files) and a list of genes to consider in analysis (ex: all intron containing genes in a text file). featureCounts outputs include the number of counts for split reads as well as the location of the split. Split reads are considered for genes that are known to contain an intron (see Ares Intron Database). The canonical SS are considered to be the 5' SS and 3' SS locations of the split read with the highest number of counts in WT samples. Within a gene, split reads are filtered for (1) a fixed 5' SS with a variable 3' SS, (2) a fixed 3' SS and a variable 5' SS, or (3) variable 5' SS and 3' SS. The same split must exist in either both WT or both Sample replicates to be further analyzed. Ratios (FAnS) are calculated from the counts for noncanonical split reads divided by the counts for the canonical split reads. FaNS are plotted on a log scale with WT ratios on the x-axis and Sample ratios on the y-axis (output #1; plots for each category of split reads). FaNS ratios are calculated from the log of Sample FaNS divided by the log of WT FaNS. The FaNS ratios are sorted into buckets and plotted as a violin plot (output #2; single plot). FAnS ratios are plotted in catagories (x-axis) based on SS nucleotides against the FAnS ratio (y-axis) (output #3; single plot). Nucleotide sequence surrounding the 5' SS and 3' SS that are found to be more frequently or less frequently used in the Sample condition are plotted as sequence logos (output #4; plots for each category). Sequence logos for canonical genes also represented in the data set are also plotted.
